feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Hide columns in Overall Release of Purchase Requisitions screen via My Inbox app
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I would like to seek for advice on how to hide a column in the Overall Release of Purchase Requisitions screen in My Inbox app. I am able to add 4 columns (Asset Number, Internal Number, GL Account and Cost Center) by using CDS View extension. After adding the columns, I was requested to hide one column (Connected Material) as the table now looks squished and that particular column is not in use. However, I am unable to use CDS Metadata extension because there is no annotation to allow metadata extension in the CDS C_PurRequisitionItemFs. Is there any way I could hide the column? Any advice would be appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Filters in the Solution Manager scenario views (7.2 SP10>=)
entry.content:
Hello to all;

Often, when accessing the applications in the Solution Manager scenario portfolio, some of the data is displayed in views/columns that are not as efficient, and it may also be the case that users need an easier way to filter.

Section 1: I will use the example of the Custom Code Objects List application, belonging to the Custom Code Management scenario, the default filter included by default in every Fiori view is the standard filter:

What happens with this way of filtering is that it is not permanent, and users have to go through these steps every time they access the application, and it becomes tedious.

I am going to tell you a much simpler way that will allow users or ourselves to speed up the filtering function.

Section 3: We will access the filtering part indicated in section 2, and we will include the filters we consider necessary to create our view:

Section 2: Now, this filter could be modified by clicking on the Filters button and include or hide the filter fields you consider:

What happens with this way of filtering is that it is not permanent, and users have to go through these steps every time they access the application, and it becomes tedious.

I am going to tell you a much simpler way that will allow users or ourselves to speed up the filtering function.

Section 3: We will access the filtering part indicated in section 2, and we will include the filters we consider necessary to create our view:

As can be seen in the previous image, I keep the filters of the standard view (as discussed in Section 2, the standard filters could be excluded if they are not part of our custom view) and include a custom filter, as can be seen the nomenclature of the standard view, currently contains an asterisk unlike if we keep the filters of the standard view, which means that we are no longer using the standard view, but it is a temporary view, made up of the filters considered.

From this point on, the customised view could be managed in the following way.

Section 4: Once we have adjusted the fields that will make up our custom view, we will click on the ‘Go’ button, which will add our field to the temporary view:

Once we have reached this point, we can include more specific data in each field as long as what we want is a customised view with specific data to consult, or save the view to make it permanent without including specific data because what we want is for the view to include the selected customised fields, but not specific data. (In any case it is not worrying, given that once our view is saved it can be updated in the future based on what we consider necessary).

To save the view is as simple as the following:

Once we have identified that we are in the temporary view, which includes our custom fields, we must click on the drop-down arrow next to the name of the view, in this case ‘Standard’, once it has been deployed we can see all the existing views, and we have at our disposal. To save our view, just click on the ‘Save As’ button.

Once the ‘Save As’ button has been clicked, the following pop-up will open:

In the Pop_up we will have to define the name that our view will receive, and the possibility of it being the standard view (Set as Default), as well as the possibility of making it public (Public).

I will detail the impact of both possibilities.

Section 5: If the ‘set as default’ flag is selected, we as users, when accessing the application in which we have defined our custom view, will see this new view as standard. If you select the flag to make the view public, it will mean that the rest of the users will have it in the list of available views (it does not mean that they can see it by default in the previous list of available views, they will have to select it as favourite, this will be discussed in the next section in more detail).

Once you have finished defining the ‘publication’ details of your view, you can save the view.

If it is necessary to include the view in a transport order because we have more than one Solution Manager in our system landscape, it will be necessary to open the client of the Solution Manager where the view is being created, and in this case when we have defined the options and nomenclature of our view, a Pop-Up will appear to indicate the transport order that will contain our view (this order must be created prior to the creation of the view).

Section 6: Select view previously defined as favourite view, so that it can be selected more easily, for them we must follow the following steps (in my case my custom view I have called it ‘Use of objects’ and I have created it so that it is public)

When a user accesses for the first time to the drop-down list of views, only the standard one will appear

In order for the user to be able to select the view we have previously created, it will be necessary to click on the ‘Manage’ button

The available views (created in the system and which are public) will appear, in addition to the SAP standard/default view.

In order for the user to see the view in the drop-down mentioned in the first image of section 6, it is necessary to select the view as favourite from the view manager, by clicking on the star:

Once it has been selected as a favourite and the changes have been saved by pressing the ‘OK’ button, it will appear in the drop-down menu:

And in this case, each time the user accesses, the only thing he will have to do is to select the view he needs from the drop-down menu.

Section 7: Additionally, I will tell you how to delete a view if it is not necessary to use it. To do this we access again to our view manager (it is indicated in previous steps how to access), and we will have available (as long as we are the owners of the view) the following button:

And save the changes.

Note: If the view has been created in a different Solution Manager than the Solution Manager where it is being used, it will be necessary to delete the view in the source Solution Manager and transport the transport order to the target Solution Manager, where a view needs to be deleted

This functionality may be extensible to other Fiori applications (standard or custom) outside the SAP Solution Manager technology framework. But currently I have only found use for it within the Solution Manager applications.

Thanks to all.

 

SAP Solution Manager SAP Fiori

entry.title: dropdown in ui5 annotation
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am using the listreport option to create a custom application.

I am having troubles with it so I hope I can get some guidelines:
1.- expand large string control, is there a way to have a full width for it. It would be even better to use the editor on this form interface.

2.- Make visible only one tab content,for some reason whole content is visible at once- .

3./ control drop down behavior,   I configured the control but when I clicked the arrow the guid value appears, only during that moment, once selected, text appears.
See attachment for reference-

4.- Do you have any guidelines to upload files, 

Info:

For nro 1,  description is a largestring field type. 

{
             $Type   : 'UI.CollectionFacet',
             ID      : 'collectionFacetSection',
             Label   : 'Treatment',
             Facets  : [
                    {
                        $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
                        ID : 'facet01',
                        Label : 'General Information',
                        Target : '@UI.FieldGroup#Group1',
                    },
                    {
                        $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
                        Label : 'Description',                        
                        Target : '@UI.FieldGroup#Group2',
                    },
             ]
        },    
 
For nro 3:
I use this to change to drop down:
speciality @Common.ValueList : {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
                CollectionPath : 'SpecialitiesSet',
                Parameters : [
                    {
                        $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                        LocalDataProperty : speciality_ID,
                        ValueListProperty : 'ID',
                    },
                    {
                        $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
                        ValueListProperty : 'name',
                    }
                ],
    };  

 

 

Thanks you for your response !

entry.title: API from SOLMAN Charm to pull data to custom dashboard
entry.content:
Hi Team


We are looking for an Api to pull the data of number of CHARM documents that are moved to production to our external dashboard


Can you let us know if SOLMAN has any such capability to pull data to integrate to other dashboards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Even I'm facing same Issue for UI_HCMORGCHART OData services.
entry.content: The entity '$AE_UIX#SRVD#UI_HCMORGCHART~SAP__HierarchySet' was not found.

entry.title: BAS in BTP Trial Account is NOT getting STARTED
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Greetings!

Yesterday, while working on the BAS (Business Application Studio) in my SAP BTP Trial account (1263e22btrial) suddenly the BAS stopped responding. I was using an incognito window for my BTP login and I thought that I might have been logged out from BTP so I refreshed the BTP Cockpit and the corresponding BAS link (https://1263e22btrial.us10cf.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/index.html). At the time I tried to STOP and START my SAP Fiori type (Fiori_Dev) Dev Space (ID: ws-lkc4q) in which I was working but it was NOT coming to STOPPED status and was just showing STOPPING instead. So, I restarted the browser and tried in a new incognito window again but this time I was NOT able to set it to START state. And since then it just shows STARTING incessantly whenever I am trying to start this Dev Space. Please refer the attached screenshot for further details.

Since I encountered this issue yesterday I have tried to set my SAP Fiori type (Fiori_Dev) Dev Space to START state in different machines and in different browsers (Chrome and Edge) but still this Dev Space (ID: ws-lkc4q) is inaccessible to me. Also, I tried to create another Dev Space (Mobile_Dev) of SAP Mobile App type (ID: ws-5gv69) and I am able to start and stop this Dev Space without any issues.

Please help me in resolving this issue and setting my Fiori_Dev Dev Space (ID: ws-lkc4q) to START state at the earliest possible as the last interval of my trial extension will expire in next 7 days.

Thanks,

Rohit V.

entry.title: Fiori Leave Request App – Default Date Always Set to Tomorrow instead of Today
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently using SAP Fiori for managing absence requests in our S/4HANA On-Premise system, and it's working well overall. However, we face a small but frustrating issue with the default date behavior.

When employees are submitting absence requests (for example home office), the system automatically sets the next day’s date (i.e., tomorrow) as the default date. This is fine if someone is planning ahead, but it's really inconvenient when submitting the request at the end of the current day.

For instance, if I try to submit an absence for today (April 7th), the system always defaults to April 8th, requiring me to manually change the date to today.

We would like to change this default behavior so that the system always proposes today’s date instead of the next day.

I checked SPRO Configuration: 

Here, we have the following options for "Proposal of next absence date":

The D option currently results in tomorrow’s date being suggested, which is causing the issue.

Has anyone come across this issue? Is there a way to configure SAP Fiori or adjust via a BAdI to set today’s date as the default proposal instead of tomorrow’s date?


Thank you in advance

Best regards

entry.title: Sorry, the draft cannot be activated right now. Please try again later.
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm trying to add custom field in FIORI apps Manage Revenue Contract, after I add my custom field from Adapt UI function and click activate, got pop up error saying "Sorry, the draft cannot be activated right now. Please try again later." 

I try to follow this solution but I cannot found any asterisk(*).

Appreciate your help on this. Thanks

entry.title: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library Unavailable due to Outdated SAPUI5 Version?
entry.content:
Hi,

It seems that the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library is currently unavailable due to an outdated version of SAPUI5. When trying to access the app via https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/, it fails to load and appears to be stuck in a loading state.

A quick look at the network traffic revealed that the app attempts to load the file https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.96.27/resources/sap-ui-core.js, but this version of SAPUI5 is outdated and no longer exists under this url, resulting in a 404 NOT FOUND error.

Could someone at SAP kindly update the SAPUI5 version in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library so we can use it again?

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Hierarchy ID Not Recognized in Balance Sheet and Income Statement (Multidimensional) App
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts,


I have created a Financial Statement Version using the Manage Global Hierarchies app. I added a node named "Income Statement" and placed three Functional Areas below it. I saved and activated the hierarchy.


However, when I go to the Balance Sheet and Income Statement (Multidimensional) app, I do not find the hierarchy ID in the selection options. When I manually enter it, I receive an error message (as shown in the image attached).

What could be the problem, and how can I solve it?

Best regards

entry.title: After kernel upgarde fiori tiles not loaded.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone.

After the kernel upgrade, we are facing some issues in the Fiori app. In the Fiori app, tiles are not opened properly. That means,

If I click the tile, only the loading symbol appears. After clicking the refresh button, it shows. Without clicking the refresh button, the tile is not opened.

entry.title: Issue in enabling the Horizontal scrollbar in the Sap Smart table
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I got a requirement to enable the horizontal scrollbar for the smart table and columns & values should not wrap inside the smart table ? 

Can someone suggest how can i achieve it ? tableType of the smart table is "Table".

I have tried keeping the smart table inside the scroll container but nothing happening but when i keep the table which is inside the Smart table in scroll container its coming as below

Please can someone throw light on this.

entry.title: After kernel upgrade, fiori tiles not loaded
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

After kernel upgrade, we are facing some issues in fiori app. Tiles not opened properly. That is If I click the tile, loaded symbol only appear, but clicking the refresh button tile will show.

without clicking the refresh button, why tiles not opened.

entry.title: UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation does not navigate to selected Sales Document
entry.content:
I am using the below annotation in a UI.LineItem annotation (i.e., a table). The annotation below is creating a button, that when clicked, navigates to the app with the semantic object of SalesContract with the action of change, which is the Change Contract - VA42 app. The RequiresContext allows me to select a row before navigating to it, which should select the SalesDocument I wish to navigate to, however, whenever I click on a row, and then hit the "Change Contract" button created by this annotation, it always navigates to one specific SalesDocument, regardless of which row I selected. Does anyone know how I would fix this issue?

<Record Type="UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation">
    <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="Change Contract"/>
    <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="SalesContract"/>
    <PropertyValue Property="Action" String="change"/>
    <PropertyValue Property="RequiresContext" Bool="true"/>
</Record>

entry.title: Smart Table fields not become editable when I press the edit button different behavior on versions
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a question : In SAP BTP I use 2 versions locally of an SAP Smart Table application - In the 1.127.0 version I use the same code as in the 1.96.38 version : 

<mvc:View controllerName="..."  

    xmlns="sap.f"

    xmlns:m="sap.m"

    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"

    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"

    xmlns:smartFilterBar="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar"

    xmlns:smartTable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"

    xmlns:sv="sap.ui.comp.smartvariants"

    xmlns:sf="sap.ui.comp.smartfield"

    xmlns:form="sap.ui.comp.smartform"

    xmlns:app="http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1"

    height="100%">

 

     <DynamicPage id="dynamicPage" fitContent="true">

        <header>

            <DynamicPageHeader id="_IDGenDynamicPageHeader1">

                <smartFilterBar:SmartFilterBar id="smartFilterBar" entitySet="ZEWM_C_MATERIAL" persistencyKey="SmartTableAnalytical_Explored"  enableBasicSearch="true">

                    <smartFilterBar:layoutData>

                        <m:FlexItemData id="_IDGenFlexItemData1" shrinkFactor="0"/>

                    </smartFilterBar:layoutData>

                </smartFilterBar:SmartFilterBar>

            </DynamicPageHeader>

        </header>

        <content>

            <smartTable:SmartTable id="LineItemsSmartTable" entitySet="ZEWM_C_MATERIAL" smartFilterId="smartFilterBar" tableType="ResponsiveTable"  

                enableExport="true" beforeExport="onBeforeExport" useVariantManagement="true" useTablePersonalisation="true" header="{i18n>smartTableHeader}"  

                showRowCount="true" persistencyKey="SmartTableAnalytical_Explored" enableAutoBinding="true"  class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding"  

                enableAutoColumnWidth="true" editTogglable="true" showDetailsButton="true" detailsButtonSetting="Medium,Low" demandPopin="true"  

                app:useSmartField = "true" editToggled="onEditToggled" placeToolbarInTable="true">  

                    <smartTable:customToolbar>

                        <m:Toolbar id="_IDGenToolbar" >

                            <m:ToolbarSpacer id="_IDGenToolbarSpacer1" ></m:ToolbarSpacer>

                            <m:Button icon="sap-icon://create" enabled = "false" id="_IDGenButton" press="onCreate" text="{i18n>Create}"></m:Button>

                            <m:Button icon="sap-icon://request" enabled = "false" id="_MassChange" press="onMassChange" text="{i18n>MassChange}"></m:Button>

                        </m:Toolbar>

                    </smartTable:customToolbar>

                    <smartTable:layoutData>

                        <m:FlexItemData id="_IDGenFlexItemData2" growFactor="1" baseSize="0%"/>

                    </smartTable:layoutData>  

                    <m:Table id="_IDGenTable" growing="true" growingThreshold="20" growingScrollToLoad="true" mode="MultiSelect"  sticky="ColumnHeaders,HeaderToolbar" />

                </smartTable:SmartTable>

        </content>

    </DynamicPage>  

</mvc:View>

The difference is that on the 1.127.0 version when I press the Edit/toggle button the fields are becoming editable and can add values on them, and in the 1.96.38 version the fields are not becoming editable when I press the button and the ones that have no value are showing just a field with a line. I know that the  app:useSmartField = "true" may be not compatible, but do you know any workaround to solve this issue please ?

entry.title: Relationship between space and Business Catalog
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Let me ask a question about the figure in the following URL.
Managing Launchpad Spaces and Pages | SAP Help Portal

In the diagram, Space and Business Catalog are associated with Business Role.
I would like to know what happens if a tile included in Space/Page is not included in the catalog.

・It is not displayed on the page
・It is displayed on the page, but there is no permission to execute it
・It is displayed on the page and can also be executed

Best Regards,
Atsushi Yamada

entry.title: Can I have a scroll bar for sap.m.tree in Mobile devices?
entry.content:
I tried to enclose the tree in a scroll container

<ScrollContainer vertical="true" width="auto" height="{parts: [{path: 'device>/resize/height'}], formatter: '.editHeightOfVSB'}">

The height will be around 80% percent of the container. With this code, I could see two scroll bars while running app in BAS. But in the mobile, I don't see the scroll bars.

The scrolling works but I need to show the bars to let the user know where he is at. 
Thanks for the help in advance.

entry.title: Implement Treasury Executive Dashboard fiori app in on-premise SAP system
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need information regarding the implementation of the "Treasury Executive dashboard" app. The OData service(/SSB/SMART_BUSINESS_RUNTIME_SRV) and UI5 application(SACINTEGS1) are already in active state in the system. However, as mentioned in the Fiori Apps Library, it is a public cloud-based app. Is there any way to integrate this app with an on-premise system?

entry.title: Undefined new sap.ui.layout.form.SimpleForm
entry.content:
Hi,

I have defined the SimpleForm control as below and it has always worked in Neo.

I am testing the same app / control in Foundry Dev subaccount and also works, however when testing in Pre-Prod subaccount sometimes it works and sometimes I have the error: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'SimpleForm').

 
var dialog = new sap.m.Dialog({
                title: sMsgYA,
                type: "Message",
                state: "Success",
                content: [
                    new sap.ui.layout.form.SimpleForm({
                        editable: false,
 
Does anybody know what the error might be?.
Thanks,
Javier

entry.title: SAP Fiori App F0106A SICF service missing
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have upgraded from S4 2018 to 2023, SAP Fiori App F0106A SICF service missing /sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/fin_receivables is missing .

Kindly help me to resolve the issue.

 

Best Regards,

Supreeth
